# how to talk to robots

> Free, open-source AI literacy resource. Plain-English guides, prompts, and
> frameworks that help beginners — teachers, parents, small business owners,
> and the simply curious — use AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude as thinking
> partners, not shortcuts. Built by educators and engineers.

## Core resources
- [Resource Library](https://www.talktorobots.org/library/): every guide, prompt, and framework, tagged by topic, audience, and type
- [For Teachers](https://www.talktorobots.org/guides/teachers/): top uses of ChatGPT & Claude in the classroom
- [For Parents](https://www.talktorobots.org/guides/parents/): AI for homework help, planning, and learning together
- [For Small Business](https://www.talktorobots.org/guides/business/): AI for emails, marketing, and admin work
- [The Socratic Peerbot](https://www.talktorobots.org/peerbot/): a framework that turns any AI chatbot into a critical-thinking partner

## Blog
- [Blog](https://www.talktorobots.org/blog/): articles, ideas, and stories about using AI as a thinking partner

## About
- [About](https://www.talktorobots.org/about/): mission, team, and how the site is built
- [Contribute](https://www.talktorobots.org/contribute/): submit a prompt or guide
- Community co-created with Third Space (thirdspaced.com). Content is free to use and share.
